qualifications.name: Diploma in Chemical Engineering
notes: Department: Chemical Engineering.
APS score: mainstream: 30; ecp: 27-29; waitlist: 23-26.
APS method: Method 2.
National certificate requirements: Mathematics N4: 60%; Engineering Science N4: 60%.
NCV requirements: English level 5; Mathematics level 5; Engineering Science level 3 with 60%.

Faculty notes: Mathematical Literacy is not accepted within the Faculty of Engineering and the Built Environment, except for Clothing and Textile Technology; National Senior Certificate must be certified by Umalusi; Some programmes use Method 2 for APS calculation; Some ECP programmes admit based on space availability; Meeting minimum requirements does not automatically imply acceptance; First option for admission, selection and placement will be given; Pre-2008 matriculants must contact the faculty office for information.

ECP consideration notes: Subject to space availability, ECP consideration may be given with English level 4; For selected Engineering ECP programmes, one of Mathematics or Physical Science may be level 4, while the other may be level 3 with at least 45%; For Technical Mathematics or Technical Science, one may be level 5 and the other level 4 with at least 55%; This ECP consideration applies to Diploma in Chemical Engineering, Diploma in Civil Engineering, Diploma in Industrial Engineering, Diploma in Mechanical Engineering, Diploma in Engineering Technology in Electrical Engineering, and Diploma in Engineering Technology in Computer Engineering.
Abbreviations: M: Mathematics; ML: Mathematical Literacy; TM: Technical Mathematics; ES: Engineering Science; PS: Physical Science; ET: Electrical Technology; TS: Technical Science.
